headline: How The Oportun Financial (OPRT) Story Is Shifting As JPMorgan Moves To Neutral
summary: Oportun Financial’s latest update keeps the model fair value steady at US$8.33, while JPMorgan maintains its US$5.50 price target. That unchanged target, paired with an upgrade to Neutral, reflects a view that recent share underperformance has pulled the stock more in line with other consumer finance names, rather than signaling a stronger conviction on upside. Read on to see how this more balanced stance fits into the broader analyst narrative and what it could mean for your own...

headline: SpaceX lines up April investor briefings as IPO questions swirl
summary: (Bloomberg) -- SpaceX is telling prospective IPO investors to expect briefings in April from company executives, according to people familiar with the matter, as advisers scramble to file for potentially the biggest listing of all time.Billionaire Elon Musk’s space and artificial intelligence company is set to hold so-called testing-the-waters investor meetings in the weeks after the Easter holiday, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. headline: Robinhood Buyback And Credit Line Tested By Valuation Concerns
summary: Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD) has authorized a new $1.5b share repurchase program to be carried out over three years, with no fixed expiration date. The company has also secured a $3.25b credit facility from JPMorgan to support the buyback and provide additional financial flexibility. The moves come as the broker faces weaker crypto trading revenue and a sharply lower share price in 2026. For investors watching Robinhood at $72.54 per share, the new buyback sits against a mixed...

headline: How The Tempus AI (TEM) Narrative Is Shifting On Growth Potential Versus Execution Risk
summary: Tempus AI sits in the middle of a wide price target range, with some recent bullish initiations reaching up to US$100 per share while others have shifted expectations into the US$60 to US$70 band. That spread reflects the split in analyst commentary, where enthusiasm around precision oncology and a reported US$1.1b data backlog is balanced against concerns about execution risks and what JPMorgan calls "clouded visibility" on data upside. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets...

headline: Wall Street Bonus Pool Jumps to a Record $49.2 Billion for 2025
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Average Wall Street bonuses jumped to a record last year, with the total pool for payouts rising to $49.2 billion as profits and revenues soared.The average annual bonus rose 6%, to $246,900, according to estimates by New York State Comptroller Thomas DiNapoli released Thursday.The total pool is the largest in records going back to 1987, reflecting a rebound in mergers and acquisitions helped by relaxed regulations under President Donald Trump. headline: Fiserv Reshapes Fintech Role With Western Alliance Deal And JP Morgan Hires
summary: Fiserv (NasdaqGS:FISV) has entered its largest ever agent bank partnership with Western Alliance Bank, expanding its merchant technology reach in the Western US. The company is hiring senior leaders from JP Morgan to support growth plans in payments, embedded finance, and generative AI. These moves point to a broad transformation of Fiserv’s business focus and service offering across financial technology. Fiserv, trading at $56.64, is taking on a bigger role in selling merchant technology...

headline: Google Breakthrough Spurs Chip Selloff Despite Analyst Doubt
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.SK Hynix Inc., a key maker of memory chips for artificial intelligence applications, fell as much as 6.4% on the Korea Exchange. Flash memory manufacturer Kioxia Holdings Corp. dropped 6.4% in Tokyo. That followed losses by Micron Technology Inc. and Sandisk Corp. Wednesday in New York.T

headline: BlackRock’s Kapito Warns Investors Are Mispricing Iran Risks
summary: (Bloomberg) -- BlackRock Inc. President Rob Kapito said investors may be underestimating the risks stemming from the Iran war, which are likely to weigh on growth and drive inflation higher even if the conflict ends soon.Growth could be hit by as much as two percentage points, while inflation may rise by a similar margin even if the war ends shortly, warned Kapito at the Asia Pacific Financial and Innovation Symposium in Melbourne on Thursday.Oil may still spike to $150 a barrel even “if we anno
